failedAssertions.reason: The auto-discover pipeline could not be completed because applying transcript-derived personas failed before the pack could be published with discovered axes.
The proposed auto-discovered axes were not schema-valid before manual edits: feeSurpriseSensitivity, automationReliance, liveHelpPreference, needsChargeExplanationClarity, checkoutFrictionTolerance.
Navigating away and back during extraction did not preserve the visible in-progress state.
When extraction fully failed, the UI showed an error state and Start over but did not provide the contract's retry action; no transcript-derived persona was created.
Partial failure surfaced per-transcript success/failure status, but the UI did not offer the contract's retry-failed/proceed controls.
Re-run extraction could not be validated after auto-discover because the apply step failed before any successful reviewed result set could be preserved for replacement.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded transcript-extraction validation knowledge: auto-discover apply mismatch, missing visible progress persistence, and the current re-run extraction limitation.

executionNotes: Task-based user-testing-flow-validator subagents could not be launched because the inherited model identifier custom:GPT-5.4-(xHigh)-16 was rejected as invalid, so the validator executed the planned browser flows directly with temporary Playwright automation against the live app.

For the main transcript-extraction validation account, keeping a persistent browser profile was necessary because data created in one signed-in session was more reliable than re-logging into the same account later.
